title: SOAP-Based Web Services
text: SOAP-Based Web Services Uniface provides the facilities for creating and using SOAP-based web services. WSDL import and export functionality for SOAP-based web services. WSDL files can be imported as Uniface signatures for call-out, or generated so that clients can call-in to Uniface web services. For more information, see WSDL . Pre-defined callback operations that are called before or after a SOAP request is handled. The SOAP callback operations make it possible to add or interpret SOAP header elements, encrypt or decrypt parts of the SOAP envelope, provide SOAP fault error handling, or perform other desired processing of the SOAP envelope. For more information, see SOAP Callback Operations . The Uniface SOAP connector, which prepares and interprets the SOAP messages. For more information, see SOAP Connector . The public interfaces and bindings of SOAP-based web services are defined and described using XML, and this definition can be discovered by other software systems. Other systems can interact with the web service in a manner prescribed by its definition, using XML-based messages conveyed by Internet protocols such as HTTP. The SOAP architecture requires the use of XML for communication and WSDL ( Web Services Description Language) for describing how the web service works. SOAP provides standardized error handling, WS-SECURITY, and transaction integrity. SOAP-based web services are typically used in enterprise and business-to-business applications. The SOAP protocol provides error reporting so that if a problem occurs with a request, the response contains error information that you can use to fix the problem. The error reporting provides standardized codes so that it's possible to automate some error handling tasks in your code. Web services that use SOAP are often stateful and complex. They are sometimes referred to as big web services or arbitrary web services. They have the following characteristics: Communication is handled by requests and responses that are sent as SOAP messages (the activation protocol) over HTTP or HTTPS (the transfer protocol). The public interfaces, including data definitions, operations and parameters, are exposed in a Web Service Description Language (WSDL) file. They can be discovered by other software systems via a UDDI (Universal Description Discovery and Integration) directory. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
